title: Why AI's environmental footprint won't disappear into orbit
excerpt: AI companies increasingly want to move their operations into space. Elon Musk's SpaceX has applied to launch over a million AI data center satellites and ultimately build them on the moon.
content_html:
AI companies increasingly want to move their operations into space. Elon Musk's SpaceX has applied to launch over a million AI data center satellites and ultimately build them on the moon.

Jeff Bezos, Google, OpenAI and others have similar plans. The idea is to harness 24/7 solar energy and, in Bezos's vision at least, to move all polluting industries off-world, rezoning Earth for humans and nature.

It's an intriguing plan. But once you factor in the whole life cycle, from water and rocket fuel used to launch these satellites into space to keeping them cool and eventually disposing of old satellites, the environmental case becomes less clear.

Outer space, out of mind

Off-worlding polluting industries is not a new idea. In the 1970s, when the last Iranian oil crisis caused rocketing fuel prices, NASA kicked off a plan to build huge solar plants in space. The idea flopped when the oil began to flow again.

NASA also explored the prospect of storing nuclear materials on the moon and blasting hazardous waste at the sun. These plans may have been feasible from an engineering perspective but were politically impossible because of the risk of radioactive particles eventually falling back to Earth.

Since data centers need so much energy, much of it for AI, such astronomical ideas are back on the table. Regular Earth-based data centers were responsible for around 5% of all electricity used in the U.S. last year, for instance, mostly using fossil fuels. Their share of U.S. power demand will likely triple by 2028.

The fragile frontier

That's why the cold vacuum of space, with its endless supply of solar energy, is being pitched as a more sustainable alternative to big, noisy, hot data centers in our backyards.

Meanwhile, a recent petition from environmental law charity Earthjustice is calling for a comprehensive review. They argue these proposals fail to acknowledge any environmental impact. "This is not just poor planning and a missed opportunity," the petition states, "it violates federal law."

Indeed, SpaceX's planning applications come with assurances of no meaningful environmental impact. "It's always sunny in space," according to Musk, who says orbital data centers are "obviously the only way to scale."

Yet data centers in space would be even more prone to overheating than those on Earth. That's because, like a Thermos flask, space keeps things hot. There's no water or air to carry excess heat away. Instead, keeping powerful AI chips cool in orbit requires massive ammonia-filled radiators that let heat escape as infrared radiation. This isn't very efficient, and those radiators are much larger than the computers they cool. Lifting these huge structures to orbit would require a lot of rocket fuel.

Most plans for orbital data centers depend on the future success of SpaceX's heavy-lift Starship. But every Starship launch burns over 1,000 metric tons of liquid methane, releasing around 80,000 metric tons of CO₂ (about the same as 20,000 cars emit in a year). The warming associated with soot emissions in the upper atmosphere is 500 times more intense than the same emissions at ground level. We know soot from rockets reduces stratospheric ozone—the stuff shielding us from harmful UV rays. In the lower atmosphere, most soot falls back to Earth in weeks. In the stratosphere, soot stays there for up to four years, prolonging its damaging effects.

While it is always sunny in space, solar panels degrade much more quickly there. Microchips are also easily damaged, falling prey to a phenomenon known as "bit flipping"—when high-energy particles rewrite memory hardware, randomly changing data from 1 to 0. Retrieving busted hardware from space is not financially viable. SpaceX satellites have a five-year shelf life before being incinerated as they fall back to Earth. The environmental impacts of things burning up in the atmosphere are not well known.

Musk's AI satellites would be connected by lasers. Should any one of the million or so satellites become untethered and collide with another, it would create further debris, creating more risk of collisions, more debris and so on. Scientists call this the Kessler syndrome and worry that orbital debris fields could hinder human activities in space for a very long time.

Orbital data centers would have a huge water footprint, too. To catch toxic dust and protect their concrete launch pad from heat damage, a typical spaceport uses a deluge system releasing around 2 million liters of water with every launch. SpaceX has repeatedly broken environmental laws with its deluge system, allowing contaminated runoff from Starship launches to enter protected waters off the coast of Texas.

Some suggest these sci-fi schemes are designed to prop up SpaceX's teetering stock price, rather than humanity's computing needs. Others see orbital data centers as just one of many ambitious visions to tackle the environmental costs of AI. Either way, my research suggests this quest to off-world the industry's headaches will have profound implications that are not well understood.

More information

Peter Howson, Extra terra nullius: Off-worlding the externalities of AI, Bitcoin mining and cloud computing with Orbital Data Centres, Energy Research & Social Science (2026). DOI: 10.1016/j.erss.2026.104725

Who's behind this story?

Sadie Harley

BSc Life Sciences & Ecology. Microbiology lab background with pharmaceutical news experience in oil, gas, and renewable industries. Full profile →

Andrew Zinin

Master's in physics with research experience. Long-time science news enthusiast. Plays key role in Science X's editorial success. Full profile →

<figcaption>
                A concept image from Nasa’s 1978 proposal for a 10km-wide ‘satellite power system’. The space-based solar farm is visible top right, while the energy would be beamed down to a ‘rectenna farm’ on earth, in the centre of the image. Credit: Nasa / US DoE / Satellite Power System (SPS) Concept Development and Evaluation Program Plan, 1978.
            </figcaption>

sentences.en: A concept image from Nasa’s 1978 proposal for a 10km-wide ‘satellite power system’.
sentences.zh: 美国宇航局 1978 年提出的 10 公里宽“卫星电力系统”提案的概念图。

sentences.en: The space-based solar farm is visible top right, while the energy would be beamed down to a ‘rectenna farm’ on earth, in the centre of the image.
sentences.zh: 太空太阳能发电场在右上角可见，而能量将被传送到地球上位于图像中心的“整流天线发电场”。

sentences.en: Credit: Nasa / US DoE / Satellite Power System (SPS) Concept Development and Evaluation Program Plan, 1978.
sentences.zh: 图片来源：NASA / 美国能源部 / 卫星动力系统 (SPS) 概念开发和评估计划计划，1978 年。

blocks.html_en: AI companies increasingly want to move their operations into space. Elon Musk's SpaceX has applied to launch over a million AI data center satellites and ultimately build them on the moon.

sentences.en: AI companies increasingly want to move their operations into space.
sentences.zh: 人工智能公司越来越希望将其业务转移到太空。

sentences.en: Elon Musk's SpaceX has applied to launch over a million AI data center satellites and ultimately build them on the moon.
sentences.zh: 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的 SpaceX 已申请发射超过 100 万颗人工智能数据中心卫星，并最终将其建造在月球上。

blocks.html_en: Jeff Bezos, Google, OpenAI and others have similar plans. The idea is to harness 24/7 solar energy and, in Bezos's vision at least, to move all polluting industries off-world, rezoning Earth for humans and nature.

sentences.en: Jeff Bezos, Google, OpenAI and others have similar plans.
sentences.zh: 杰夫·贝佐斯、谷歌、OpenAI 和其他公司也有类似的计划。

sentences.en: The idea is to harness 24/7 solar energy and, in Bezos's vision at least, to move all polluting industries off-world, rezoning Earth for humans and nature.
sentences.zh: 这个想法是利用 24/7 太阳能，至少在贝索斯的愿景中，将所有污染工业移出地球，为人类和自然重新规划地球。

blocks.html_en: It's an intriguing plan. But once you factor in the whole life cycle, from water and rocket fuel used to launch these satellites into space to keeping them cool and eventually disposing of old satellites, the environmental case becomes less clear.

sentences.en: It's an intriguing plan.
sentences.zh: 这是一个有趣的计划。

sentences.en: But once you factor in the whole life cycle, from water and rocket fuel used to launch these satellites into space to keeping them cool and eventually disposing of old satellites, the environmental case becomes less clear.
sentences.zh: 但是，一旦考虑到整个生命周期，从用于将这些卫星发射到太空的水和火箭燃料，到保持卫星冷却并最终处置旧卫星，环境问题就变得不那么清晰了。

blocks.html_en: Outer space, out of mind

sentences.en: Outer space, out of mind
sentences.zh: 外太空，心不在焉

blocks.html_en: Off-worlding polluting industries is not a new idea. In the 1970s, when the last Iranian oil crisis caused rocketing fuel prices, NASA kicked off a plan to build huge solar plants in space. The idea flopped when the oil began to flow again.

sentences.en: Off-worlding polluting industries is not a new idea.
sentences.zh: 远离世界污染的工业并不是一个新想法。

sentences.en: In the 1970s, when the last Iranian oil crisis caused rocketing fuel prices, NASA kicked off a plan to build huge solar plants in space.
sentences.zh: 20 世纪 70 年代，当上一次伊朗石油危机导致燃料价格飙升时，美国宇航局 (NASA) 启动了一项在太空建造大型太阳能发电厂的计划。

sentences.en: The idea flopped when the oil began to flow again.
sentences.zh: 当石油再次开始流动时，这个想法失败了。

blocks.html_en: NASA also explored the prospect of storing nuclear materials on the moon and blasting hazardous waste at the sun. These plans may have been feasible from an engineering perspective but were politically impossible because of the risk of radioactive particles eventually falling back to Earth.

sentences.en: NASA also explored the prospect of storing nuclear materials on the moon and blasting hazardous waste at the sun.
sentences.zh: 美国宇航局还探索了在月球上储存核材料和在太阳上爆炸危险废物的前景。

sentences.en: These plans may have been feasible from an engineering perspective but were politically impossible because of the risk of radioactive particles eventually falling back to Earth.
sentences.zh: 这些计划从工程角度来看可能是可行的，但在政治上是不可能的，因为放射性粒子最终有落回地球的风险。

blocks.html_en: Since data centers need so much energy, much of it for AI, such astronomical ideas are back on the table. Regular Earth-based data centers were responsible for around 5% of all electricity used in the U.S. last year, for instance, mostly using fossil fuels. Their share of U.S. power demand will likely triple by 2028.

sentences.en: Since data centers need so much energy, much of it for AI, such astronomical ideas are back on the table.
sentences.zh: 由于数据中心需要大量能源，其中大部分用于人工智能，因此这种天文数字的想法又回到了桌面上。

sentences.en: Regular Earth-based data centers were responsible for around 5% of all electricity used in the U.S. last year, for instance, mostly using fossil fuels.
sentences.zh: 例如，去年，常规地球数据中心的用电量约占美国总用电量的 5%，其中大部分使用化石燃料。

sentences.en: Their share of U.S. power demand will likely triple by 2028.
sentences.zh: 到 2028 年，它们在美国电力需求中的份额可能会增加两倍。

blocks.html_en: The fragile frontier

sentences.en: The fragile frontier
sentences.zh: 脆弱的边境

blocks.html_en: That's why the cold vacuum of space, with its endless supply of solar energy, is being pitched as a more sustainable alternative to big, noisy, hot data centers in our backyards.

sentences.en: That's why the cold vacuum of space, with its endless supply of solar energy, is being pitched as a more sustainable alternative to big, noisy, hot data centers in our backyards.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么太空的冷真空及其源源不断的太阳能被认为是我们后院大型、嘈杂、炎热的数据中心的更可持续的替代品。

blocks.html_en: Meanwhile, a recent petition from environmental law charity Earthjustice is calling for a comprehensive review. They argue these proposals fail to acknowledge any environmental impact. "This is not just poor planning and a missed opportunity," the petition states, "it violates federal law."

sentences.en: Meanwhile, a recent petition from environmental law charity Earthjustice is calling for a comprehensive review.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，环境法慈善机构“地球正义”最近提交了一份请愿书，呼吁进行全面审查。

sentences.en: They argue these proposals fail to acknowledge any environmental impact.
sentences.zh: 他们认为这些提案没有承认任何环境影响。

sentences.en: "This is not just poor planning and a missed opportunity," the petition states, "it violates federal law."
sentences.zh: 请愿书指出：“这不仅是糟糕的计划和错失的机会，而且还违反了联邦法律。”

blocks.html_en: Indeed, SpaceX's planning applications come with assurances of no meaningful environmental impact. "It's always sunny in space," according to Musk, who says orbital data centers are "obviously the only way to scale."

sentences.en: Indeed, SpaceX's planning applications come with assurances of no meaningful environmental impact.
sentences.zh: 事实上，SpaceX 的规划申请保证不会对环境造成任何影响。

sentences.en: "It's always sunny in space," according to Musk, who says orbital data centers are "obviously the only way to scale."
sentences.zh: 马斯克表示：“太空中总是阳光明媚。”他表示，轨道数据中心“显然是扩展规模的唯一途径”。

blocks.html_en: Yet data centers in space would be even more prone to overheating than those on Earth. That's because, like a Thermos flask, space keeps things hot. There's no water or air to carry excess heat away. Instead, keeping powerful AI chips cool in orbit requires massive ammonia-filled radiators that let heat escape as infrared radiation. This isn't very efficient, and those radiators are much larger than the computers they cool. Lifting these huge structures to orbit would require a lot of rocket fuel.

sentences.en: Yet data centers in space would be even more prone to overheating than those on Earth.
sentences.zh: 然而，太空中的数据中心比地球上的数据中心更容易过热。

sentences.en: That's because, like a Thermos flask, space keeps things hot.
sentences.zh: 这是因为，就像保温瓶一样，空间可以让东西保持热度。

sentences.en: There's no water or air to carry excess heat away.
sentences.zh: 没有水或空气可以带走多余的热量。

sentences.en: Instead, keeping powerful AI chips cool in orbit requires massive ammonia-filled radiators that let heat escape as infrared radiation.
sentences.zh: 相反，要让强大的人工智能芯片在轨道上保持凉爽，需要大量充满氨的散热器，让热量以红外辐射的形式散失。

sentences.en: This isn't very efficient, and those radiators are much larger than the computers they cool.
sentences.zh: 这效率不高，而且这些散热器比它们冷却的计算机大得多。

sentences.en: Lifting these huge structures to orbit would require a lot of rocket fuel.
sentences.zh: 将这些巨大的结构提升到轨道需要大量的火箭燃料。

blocks.html_en: Most plans for orbital data centers depend on the future success of SpaceX's heavy-lift Starship. But every Starship launch burns over 1,000 metric tons of liquid methane, releasing around 80,000 metric tons of CO₂ (about the same as 20,000 cars emit in a year). The warming associated with soot emissions in the upper atmosphere is 500 times more intense than the same emissions at ground level. We know soot from rockets reduces stratospheric ozone—the stuff shielding us from harmful UV rays. In the lower atmosphere, most soot falls back to Earth in weeks. In the stratosphere, soot stays there for up to four years, prolonging its damaging effects.

sentences.en: Most plans for orbital data centers depend on the future success of SpaceX's heavy-lift Starship.
sentences.zh: 大多数轨道数据中心计划都取决于 SpaceX 重型星际飞船未来的成功。

sentences.en: But every Starship launch burns over 1,000 metric tons of liquid methane, releasing around 80,000 metric tons of CO₂ (about the same as 20,000 cars emit in a year).
sentences.zh: 但每次星舰发射都会燃烧超过 1,000 吨液态甲烷，释放约 80,000 吨二氧化碳（大约相当于 20,000 辆汽车一年的排放量）。

sentences.en: The warming associated with soot emissions in the upper atmosphere is 500 times more intense than the same emissions at ground level.
sentences.zh: 与高层大气中烟尘排放相关的变暖程度比地面相同排放量高出 500 倍。

sentences.en: We know soot from rockets reduces stratospheric ozone—the stuff shielding us from harmful UV rays.
sentences.zh: 我们知道火箭产生的烟灰会减少平流层臭氧，而臭氧可以保护我们免受有害紫外线的伤害。

sentences.en: In the lower atmosphere, most soot falls back to Earth in weeks.
sentences.zh: 在低层大气中，大多数烟灰会在几周内落回地球。

sentences.en: In the stratosphere, soot stays there for up to four years, prolonging its damaging effects.
sentences.zh: 在平流层中，烟灰会在那里停留长达四年，从而延长了其破坏性影响。

blocks.html_en: While it is always sunny in space, solar panels degrade much more quickly there. Microchips are also easily damaged, falling prey to a phenomenon known as "bit flipping"—when high-energy particles rewrite memory hardware, randomly changing data from 1 to 0. Retrieving busted hardware from space is not financially viable. SpaceX satellites have a five-year shelf life before being incinerated as they fall back to Earth. The environmental impacts of things burning up in the atmosphere are not well known.

sentences.en: While it is always sunny in space, solar panels degrade much more quickly there.
sentences.zh: 虽然太空中总是阳光明媚，但太阳能电池板的退化速度要快得多。

sentences.en: Microchips are also easily damaged, falling prey to a phenomenon known as "bit flipping"—when high-energy particles rewrite memory hardware, randomly changing data from 1 to 0.
sentences.zh: 微芯片也很容易被损坏，成为一种被称为“位翻转”的现象的牺牲品——当高能粒子重写存储器硬件时，随机地将数据从 1 更改为 0。

sentences.en: Retrieving busted hardware from space is not financially viable.
sentences.zh: 从太空取回损坏的硬件在经济上不可行。

sentences.en: SpaceX satellites have a five-year shelf life before being incinerated as they fall back to Earth.
sentences.zh: SpaceX 卫星在落回地球时被焚烧之前有五年的保质期。

sentences.en: The environmental impacts of things burning up in the atmosphere are not well known.
sentences.zh: 大气中燃烧的物质对环境的影响尚不清楚。

blocks.html_en: Musk's AI satellites would be connected by lasers. Should any one of the million or so satellites become untethered and collide with another, it would create further debris, creating more risk of collisions, more debris and so on. Scientists call this the Kessler syndrome and worry that orbital debris fields could hinder human activities in space for a very long time.

sentences.en: Musk's AI satellites would be connected by lasers.
sentences.zh: 马斯克的人工智能卫星将通过激光连接。

sentences.en: Should any one of the million or so satellites become untethered and collide with another, it would create further debris, creating more risk of collisions, more debris and so on.
sentences.zh: 如果这百万颗左右的卫星中的任何一颗脱离束缚并与另一颗相撞，就会产生更多的碎片，从而产生更大的碰撞风险、更多的碎片等等。

sentences.en: Scientists call this the Kessler syndrome and worry that orbital debris fields could hinder human activities in space for a very long time.
sentences.zh: 科学家将这种现象称为凯斯勒综合症，并担心轨道碎片场可能会在很长一段时间内阻碍人类在太空的活动。

blocks.html_en: Orbital data centers would have a huge water footprint, too. To catch toxic dust and protect their concrete launch pad from heat damage, a typical spaceport uses a deluge system releasing around 2 million liters of water with every launch. SpaceX has repeatedly broken environmental laws with its deluge system, allowing contaminated runoff from Starship launches to enter protected waters off the coast of Texas.

sentences.en: Orbital data centers would have a huge water footprint, too.
sentences.zh: 轨道数据中心也会产生巨大的水足迹。

sentences.en: To catch toxic dust and protect their concrete launch pad from heat damage, a typical spaceport uses a deluge system releasing around 2 million liters of water with every launch.
sentences.zh: 为了捕获有毒粉尘并保护混凝土发射台免受热损坏，典型的航天发射场使用雨淋系统，每次发射时释放约 200 万升水。

sentences.en: SpaceX has repeatedly broken environmental laws with its deluge system, allowing contaminated runoff from Starship launches to enter protected waters off the coast of Texas.
sentences.zh: SpaceX 的雨淋系统多次违反环境法，导致星际飞船发射时受污染的径流进入德克萨斯州海岸附近的受保护水域。

blocks.html_en: Some suggest these sci-fi schemes are designed to prop up SpaceX's teetering stock price, rather than humanity's computing needs. Others see orbital data centers as just one of many ambitious visions to tackle the environmental costs of AI. Either way, my research suggests this quest to off-world the industry's headaches will have profound implications that are not well understood.

sentences.en: Some suggest these sci-fi schemes are designed to prop up SpaceX's teetering stock price, rather than humanity's computing needs.
sentences.zh: 一些人认为，这些科幻计划的目的是支撑 SpaceX 摇摇欲坠的股价，而不是人类的计算需求。

sentences.en: Others see orbital data centers as just one of many ambitious visions to tackle the environmental costs of AI.
sentences.zh: 其他人则认为轨道数据中心只是解决人工智能环境成本的众多雄心勃勃的愿景之一。

sentences.en: Either way, my research suggests this quest to off-world the industry's headaches will have profound implications that are not well understood.
sentences.zh: 不管怎样，我的研究表明，这种解决行业难题的努力将产生尚未被充分理解的深远影响。

content_html_zh:
人工智能公司越来越希望将其业务转移到太空。 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的 SpaceX 已申请发射超过 100 万颗人工智能数据中心卫星，并最终将其建造在月球上。

杰夫·贝佐斯、谷歌、OpenAI 和其他公司也有类似的计划。 这个想法是利用 24/7 太阳能，至少在贝索斯的愿景中，将所有污染工业移出地球，为人类和自然重新规划地球。

这是一个有趣的计划。 但是，一旦考虑到整个生命周期，从用于将这些卫星发射到太空的水和火箭燃料，到保持卫星冷却并最终处置旧卫星，环境问题就变得不那么清晰了。

外太空，心不在焉

远离世界污染的工业并不是一个新想法。 20 世纪 70 年代，当上一次伊朗石油危机导致燃料价格飙升时，美国宇航局 (NASA) 启动了一项在太空建造大型太阳能发电厂的计划。 当石油再次开始流动时，这个想法失败了。

美国宇航局还探索了在月球上储存核材料和在太阳上爆炸危险废物的前景。 这些计划从工程角度来看可能是可行的，但在政治上是不可能的，因为放射性粒子最终有落回地球的风险。

由于数据中心需要大量能源，其中大部分用于人工智能，因此这种天文数字的想法又回到了桌面上。 例如，去年，常规地球数据中心的用电量约占美国总用电量的 5%，其中大部分使用化石燃料。 到 2028 年，它们在美国电力需求中的份额可能会增加两倍。

脆弱的边境

这就是为什么太空的冷真空及其源源不断的太阳能被认为是我们后院大型、嘈杂、炎热的数据中心的更可持续的替代品。

与此同时，环境法慈善机构“地球正义”最近提交了一份请愿书，呼吁进行全面审查。 他们认为这些提案没有承认任何环境影响。 请愿书指出：“这不仅是糟糕的计划和错失的机会，而且还违反了联邦法律。”

事实上，SpaceX 的规划申请保证不会对环境造成任何影响。 马斯克表示：“太空中总是阳光明媚。”他表示，轨道数据中心“显然是扩展规模的唯一途径”。

然而，太空中的数据中心比地球上的数据中心更容易过热。 这是因为，就像保温瓶一样，空间可以让东西保持热度。 没有水或空气可以带走多余的热量。 相反，要让强大的人工智能芯片在轨道上保持凉爽，需要大量充满氨的散热器，让热量以红外辐射的形式散失。 这效率不高，而且这些散热器比它们冷却的计算机大得多。 将这些巨大的结构提升到轨道需要大量的火箭燃料。

大多数轨道数据中心计划都取决于 SpaceX 重型星际飞船未来的成功。 但每次星舰发射都会燃烧超过 1,000 吨液态甲烷，释放约 80,000 吨二氧化碳（大约相当于 20,000 辆汽车一年的排放量）。 与高层大气中烟尘排放相关的变暖程度比地面相同排放量高出 500 倍。 我们知道火箭产生的烟灰会减少平流层臭氧，而臭氧可以保护我们免受有害紫外线的伤害。 在低层大气中，大多数烟灰会在几周内落回地球。 在平流层中，烟灰会在那里停留长达四年，从而延长了其破坏性影响。

虽然太空中总是阳光明媚，但太阳能电池板的退化速度要快得多。 微芯片也很容易被损坏，成为一种被称为“位翻转”的现象的牺牲品——当高能粒子重写存储器硬件时，随机地将数据从 1 更改为 0。 从太空取回损坏的硬件在经济上不可行。 SpaceX 卫星在落回地球时被焚烧之前有五年的保质期。 大气中燃烧的物质对环境的影响尚不清楚。

马斯克的人工智能卫星将通过激光连接。 如果这百万颗左右的卫星中的任何一颗脱离束缚并与另一颗相撞，就会产生更多的碎片，从而产生更大的碰撞风险、更多的碎片等等。 科学家将这种现象称为凯斯勒综合症，并担心轨道碎片场可能会在很长一段时间内阻碍人类在太空的活动。

轨道数据中心也会产生巨大的水足迹。 为了捕获有毒粉尘并保护混凝土发射台免受热损坏，典型的航天发射场使用雨淋系统，每次发射时释放约 200 万升水。 SpaceX 的雨淋系统多次违反环境法，导致星际飞船发射时受污染的径流进入德克萨斯州海岸附近的受保护水域。

一些人认为，这些科幻计划的目的是支撑 SpaceX 摇摇欲坠的股价，而不是人类的计算需求。 其他人则认为轨道数据中心只是解决人工智能环境成本的众多雄心勃勃的愿景之一。 不管怎样，我的研究表明，这种解决行业难题的努力将产生尚未被充分理解的深远影响。

更多信息

Peter Howson，《无主之地：通过轨道数据中心、能源研究和社会科学消除人工智能、比特币挖矿和云计算的外部性》（2026 年）。 DOI：10.1016/j.erss.2026.104725

谁是这个故事的幕后黑手？

萨迪·哈雷

生命科学与生态学理学士。 微生物学实验室背景，具有石油、天然气和可再生行业的制药新闻经验。 完整档案 →

安德鲁·齐宁

物理学硕士，具有研究经验。 长期科学新闻爱好者。 在 Science X 的编辑成功中发挥着关键作用。 完整档案 →
